steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Cross-Text Connections question. Text 1 presents Madhusudhan et al.'s finding that K2-18 b sits on the inner edge of the habitable zone (HZ) at about 1 AU. Text 2 presents Tsai et al.'s competing claim that hydrogen-rich atmospheres let in light that raises surface temperatures, pushing the HZ inner edge out to 3.85 AU. The question asks how Tsai et al. would most likely respond to Madhusudhan et al. — specifically, what challenge Tsai et al.'s work poses to Text 1.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'By arguing that K2-18 b and other hycean candidates are unlikely to support life because these planets are located too far from the stars they orbit.': Tsai et al. say K2-18 b is too close (inside the revised HZ), not too far. This reverses the direction of the claim. ✗
  • Choice B — 'By stating that the chemical composition of the atmosphere of the hycean candidate Madhusudhan et al. identified suggests that this planet's surface is unlikely to harbor liquid water.': Tsai et al. argue that the hydrogen-rich atmosphere admits wavelengths that raise temperatures and evaporate water — so K2-18 b (at ~1 AU) falls inside the revised inner HZ edge of 3.85 AU and cannot retain liquid water. This accurately captures their response. ✓
  • Choice C — 'By maintaining that Madhusudhan et al. relied on a model whose estimates of surface temperatures on hycean candidates are likely too high.': It is Tsai et al. who find higher surface temperatures than earlier assessments; they do not accuse Madhusudhan et al. of overestimating temperatures. ✗
  • Choice D — 'By observing that unlike the hycean candidate Madhusudhan et al. discovered, most other types of planets with hydrogen-rich atmospheres are likely located within the HZ.': Text 2 makes no such comparative claim about other planet types. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
